nameMeanings.name: d'Oyley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Olley with fused Old French preposition d' ‘from’.

variants.name: Olley
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of   Oley   2, a habitational name of Norman origin, from any of the five places called Ouilly in Calvados, France.

variants.name: Dilley
meanings: English: possibly a pet form of an unrecorded Old English personal name Dylla, found as the first element in the placenames Dillington (in the former Huntingdonshire) and Dilton (in Wiltshire). The name may sometimes have been confused with Doyley and Dolley (see Olley ).
German: variant of   Dilly   or a surname of French origin, an altered form of   Delay  .
In some cases also an altered form of French   Dilly  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas d'Oyley was born in 1321, in Oxfordshire, England, his father, Richard d'Oyley, was 35 and his mother, Elizabeth Knightly, was 22. He had at least 7 sons with Alice Atlude. He died about 1384, in Woburn, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 64.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas d'Oyley(이)가 1321년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard d'Oyley(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Knightly(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Alice Atlude(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1384년 영국 베드퍼드실르 보번에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas d'Oyley родился в 1321 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard d'Oyley было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Knightly, 22. У него было по крайней мере 7 сыновей от Alice Atlude. Он умер около 1384 г. в местности вобурн, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет.
